original_question:
A standard fair six-sided die is rolled repeatedly. Each time the die reads 1 or 2, Alice gets a coin; each time it reads 3 or 4, Bob gets a coin; and each time it reads 5 or 6, Carol gets a coin. The probability that Alice and Bob each receive at least two coins before Carol receives any coins can be written as $\tfrac{m}{n},$ where $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime positive integers. Find $100m+n.$

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: A standard fair six-sided die is rolled repeatedly. Each time the die reads 1 or 3, Alice gets a coin; each time it reads 2 or 4, Bob gets a coin; and each time it reads 5 or 6, Carol gets a coin. The probability that Alice and Bob each receive at least two coins before Carol receives any coins can be written as \(\tfrac{m}{n},\) where \(m\) and \(n\) are relatively prime positive integers. Find \(100m+n.\)

The probability that Alice and Bob each receive at least two coins before Carol receives any coins can be written as \(\tfrac{m}{n},\) where \(m\) and \(n\) are relatively prime positive integers. A standard fair six-sided die is rolled repeatedly. Each time the die reads 2 or 6, Alice gets a coin; each time it reads 3 or 5, Bob gets a coin; and each time it reads 1 or 4, Carol gets a coin. The probability that Alice and Bob each receive at least two coins before Carol receives any coins can be written as \(\tfrac{m}{n},\) where \(m\) and \(n\) are relatively prime positive integers.
